text.title: Privacy-Compliant Analytics Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `Privacy-Compliant Analytics` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: CMP MUST default to deny for analytics + advertising + functional cookies until explicit consent. Pre-checked consent boxes are forbidden (GDPR Art. 7 + 2024 EDPB guidelines).

rationale.source: GDPR Art. 7; EDPB Cookie Banner Taskforce report 2024

rule.rationale: Default-allow is illegal in EU under GDPR; CCPA also penalizes silent opt-in. Default-deny is the only configuration that survives audit.

rule.statement: Google products (GA4, Ads) MUST use Consent Mode v2 with ad_storage + analytics_storage + ad_user_data + ad_personalization signals. Consent Mode v1 is deprecated.

rationale.source: Google Consent Mode v2 docs; March 2024 enforcement

rule.rationale: Without v2, Google rejects EEA conversions starting March 2024. Without all four signals, retargeting + modeled conversions break silently.

rule.statement: Server MUST honor Global Privacy Control (GPC) header signal as a CCPA opt-out. Ignoring GPC is a documented CCPA violation as of CA Attorney General 2023 enforcement.

rationale.source: CCPA enforcement actions 2023-2024; Sephora settlement precedent

rule.rationale: GPC is a binding signal under CCPA. Major settlements (Sephora $1.2M) cite GPC ignorance specifically. Detecting + honoring header is mandatory, not optional.

rule.statement: Analytics tools MUST anonymize IP (truncate last octet for IPv4, last 80 bits for IPv6) before storage. Raw IP storage requires DPA + lawful basis.

rationale.source: GA4 docs anonymizeIp; ICO IP guidance

rule.rationale: Raw IP = personal data under GDPR. Tools that ship anonymization by default (GA4, Plausible, Matomo) need it confirmed in config; tools that don't (custom logging) need pre-storage hashing.

rule.statement: Every release MUST include a tag-firing audit: list of tags + which consent categories they require + a network-tab proof. New tags without entry are forbidden.

rationale.source: GTM audit best-practice; OneTrust deployment guides

rule.rationale: Tag drift is the #1 silent compliance break. Without per-release audit, marketing adds a pixel, dev forgets to gate it, regulator finds it on a random visit.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies If fail OR any item in Skip If triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
